checks.command: test -f standard/NDS.md && test -f standard/GIP.md && test -f standard/AGF.md && test -f standard/anti_hallucination.md && test -f standard/README.md && echo PASS
for f in standard/*.md; do test "$(head -1 "$f")" = '---'; done (5 files, all frontmatter)
NDS.md mentions all 6 required frontmatter keys
no 'nizamiq' branding in standard/ (with file-count guard against silent no-op)
standard/ contains exactly the 5 contracted files, no stray extras

adversarial_check.description: Two-part check beyond the 7 listed commands, using python3+yaml+jsonschema: (1) parsed the YAML block between the first two '---' lines of all 5 standard/*.md files and validated each against schema/frontmatter.schema.json (draft 2020-12) -- all 5 validated cleanly (id kebab-case, version semver, status in draft/active/deprecated enum, authoritative_source non-empty). (2) Cross-checked standard/AGF.md Section 4's JSON Verdict Parse Rule prose against the literal gate rule this evaluation pipeline enforces: searched AGF.md verbatim for the four fragments 'final_verdict.approved === true', 'final_verdict.issues.length === 0', 'final_verdict.missing_acceptance_coverage.length === 0', 'final_verdict.unsupported_claims.length === 0' -- all four present verbatim, confirming AGF.md's documented gate matches the actual approved/issues/missing_acceptance_coverage/unsupported_claims gate used to advance this pipeline (no drift between doc and practice).
